title: Latent degradation slope and stint reset features
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation research models tyre state as a latent process with pit stops as structural resets, and strategy models often use degradation slope/convexity rather than only tyre age.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation research models tyre state as a latent process with pit stops as structural resets, and strategy models often use degradation slope/convexity rather than only tyre age. Prior attempts used simple rolling pace drops; a more explicit per-stint state estimate is a distinct next step.
implementation_hint: Within Race-Driver-Stint ordered by LapNumber, compute lag-only expanding and rolling features: previous-lap residual vs race-lap median, rolling slope over last 3/5 laps, rolling std, acceleration of degradation, cumulative positive residual, and distance from compound-specific expected stint optimum. Avoid full-stint means that see future laps.
expected_effect: Should help identify the tyre cliff and differentiate gradual fuel-burn effects from true degradation-driven pit likelihood.
risk: Requires careful sorting and reindexing; early-stint rows have missing histories that must be filled consistently.
